title: 2 Terminology
body_text:
2	Terminology
2.1	Definitions
Authorized Official
An Authorized Official is a person legally authorized to authenticate documents in the capacity of one of the following titles (specify title on documents authorized): notary (with seal), Commissioner for Oaths, Justice of the Peace (with seal), lawyer (seal required only if also a notary on stamp used).
Authorized Redemption Agent
An Authorized Redemption Agent is authorized by the Bank of Canada to redeem all Government of Canada issued bonds.
Bearer Bond
A Bearer Bond is an unregistered bond which may be redeemed by the person in possession.
Bond Head
A Bond Head is the upper portion of a Regular Interest ГўВЂВњRГўВЂВќ or Compound Interest ГўВЂВњCГўВЂВќ Canada Savings Bond that is detached by the redemption agent at redemption. The Bond Head has no value.
Bonus Interest
A Bonus Interest is an interest earned as a result of rate increases subsequent to the purchase of the bond. Bonus interest may be declared as capital gain.
Cash Bonus ГўВЂВ“ Canada Savings Bond
A Cash Bonus ГўВЂВ“ Canada Savings Bond is a bonus payment designated by the Government of Canada on certain series of Canada Savings Bonds in addition to any Coupons or compound interest certificates.
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1ГўВЂВ™s applications and to information and news about our products and services.
Closed Book ГўВЂВ“ Canada Savings Regular Interest ГўВЂВњRГўВЂВќ Bonds
A Closed Book ГўВЂВ“ Canada Savings Regular Interest ГўВЂВњRГўВЂВќ Bonds are bonds that pay interest by cheque or direct deposit. As a result, registers are closed in preparation for payment of interest two months in advance of the annual interest payment date (November 1). Bondholders redeeming ГўВЂВњRГўВЂВќ Bonds during the months of September and October receive par value less the unearned interest for either one or two months.
Commissioner for Oaths
A Commissioner for Oaths is an official appointed by law to take affidavits.
Compound Interest ГўВЂВњCГўВЂВќ Canada Savings Bond
A Compound Interest ГўВЂВњCГўВЂВќ Canada Savings Bond is a bond registered as to principal and interest having a compound interest feature. Interest is compounded annually to maturity and payable at maturity or upon redemption.
Coupon
A Coupon is an interest-bearing certificate attached to some types of bonds. Coupons are designed to be detached and redeemed at the interest payment date printed on the Coupon.
Coupon Bond
A Coupon Bond is a bond registered as to principal which pays interest by means of attached Coupons. Some Coupon Bonds may also include compound interest certificates.
Declarant
A Declarant is a beneficiary or executor applying to transfer the registration of a bond.
Endorsement Stamp
An Endorsement Stamp is a stamp that includes the credit unionГўВЂВ™s name, branch address, and date of endorsement.
Fully Registered Bond
A Fully Registered Bond is a bond registered as to principal and interest, with interest paid directly to the Registered Owner by the issuer.
Grant of Administration
A Grant of Administration is a document granted by the superior court of the province that appoints an administrator to administer the estate of:
a person who dies Intestate (without a Will), or
a person who leaves a Will, but whose named executor is unable or unwilling to act.
The document grants the administrator the legal authority to represent the estate. The document is then known as ГўВЂВњGrant of Administration with Will annexedГўВЂВќ.
Grant of Probate
A Grant of Probate is a document granted by the superior court of the province confirming the validity of a deceased personГўВЂВ™s Will and the appointment of the executor. The document confirms the legal authority of the executor to represent the estate.
Intestate
Intestate means not having left a valid Will.
Letter of Name Guarantee
A Letter of Name Guarantee is a letter provided on credit union letterhead and signed by an authorized signing officer guaranteeing that a person known to them under a variation of names is one and the same person.
Maturity Date
The Maturity Date is the date specified on a bond (or Coupon) for redemption at full value.
MICR
Magnetic Ink Character Recognition (MICR) is the machine recognition of characters printed with ink that contains particles of a magnetic material.
Notarial Copy
A Notarial Copy is a copy of an original document, certified by a lawyer or notary public as being a true copy.
Ontario Savings Bond
An Ontario Savings Bond (OSB) is a bond security issued by the province of Ontario and sold only to residents of Ontario. The Province of Ontario backs the principal and interest of OSBs.
Original Interest
The Original Interest is an amount of interest the issuer of a bond agreed to pay at the time the bond was issued. It is taxable as interest income.
Power of Attorney
A Power of Attorney is a legal declaration that the person signing the document/bond is the registered or rightful owner of the bond or a legally appointed agent empowered to sign for the owner.
Proof of Death
A Proof of Death is the legal verification of an individualГўВЂВ™s death provided by one of the following original documents:
Death Certificate
Certificate by a Minister of Religion
Certificate of Finding of a Coroner
Certificate issued by an undertaking establishment under corporate seal
Note:	Copies of an original document must be certified by a Commissioner for Oaths or a Notary Public with the NotaryГўВЂВ™s signature and seal/stamp of office.
Succession Duty Release
Grant of Probate
Grant of Administration
Redemption Certificate
A Redemption Certificate is the lower portion of a Regular Interest ГўВЂВњRГўВЂВќ or Compound Interest ГўВЂВњCГўВЂВќ Canada Savings Bond. The Registered Owner endorses the reverse of the Redemption Certificate to authorize redemption.
Registered Owner
A Registered Owner is an owner of a bond as registered with the issuer. A Registered Owner may be one or more individuals, a company, a trust, or a nominee. Only the Registered Owner or her or his legal representative is authorized to redeem, transfer, or sell the bond.
Regular Interest ГўВЂВњRГўВЂВќ Canada Savings Bond
A Regular Interest ГўВЂВњRГўВЂВќ Canada Savings Bond is a bond registered as to principal and interest, issued by the Bank of Canada. Interest is paid annually on November 1
st
either by cheque or direct deposit to the Registered OwnerГўВЂВ™s account. Redemption includes accrued interest (if any) since the last interest payment date.
ServiceNow
ServiceNow is the web-based ticketing application used to report service issues, request changes to existing services, or submit questions to Central 1.
Testate
Testate means having left a valid Will.
Treasury Bill
A Treasury Bill is a bearer instrument issued by the Bank of Canada through tender at a discount. Treasury Bills mature at face value.
Unmatured
Unmatured means that a bond or Coupon has not reached its Maturity Date.
Will
A Will is a legal declaration by which a person, the testator, names one or more persons to manage his or her estate and provides for the distribution of his or her property at death.
END
